_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Per-batch ignore overlap & force-start

steps.description: Add `BatchOverlapIgnore` ORM model to orch/db/models.py with composite PK (project_id, batch_id, held_item_id, blocking_item_id, file_pattern) and FKs to batches and batch_items; nullable `reason` TEXT, `ignored_by` VARCHAR NOT NULL, `ignored_at` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Generate the Alembic revision via `uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "CR-00078 add batch_overlap_ignore"`. Verify the autogen produced the table create + both FKs; tighten the migration manually if autogen missed the composite FK to batch_items(project_id, batch_id, work_item_id). Do NOT apply the migration to the live DB — that is the daemon's job. Add Python-side validators only if essential.

Per-agent review of S01: model fields match the schema (PK composition, NOT NULL on five PK cols + ignored_by + ignored_at, NULL on reason); both FKs declared correctly (cascade rules: ON DELETE CASCADE for the batch_items FK is acceptable, ON DELETE NO ACTION for the batches FK to preserve audit when batches are archived); the migration's upgrade() creates the table and downgrade() drops it cleanly; no other table touched by the migration (no incidental autogen drift); the new model is registered in models.py imports so Base.metadata.create_all() picks it up in testcontainer fixtures.
QV: Alembic migration round-trip + drift check

Add a pure helper `filter_blocked_by_ignores(blocked_by, ignores) -> blocked_by'` in orch/daemon/scope_overlap.py (or a new orch/daemon/overlap_ignore.py — pick whichever keeps scope_overlap.py pure of DB knowledge). The helper takes the `list[tuple[blocking_item_id, list[file_pattern]]]` returned by find_blocking_items and a `set[tuple[blocking_item_id, file_pattern]]` of ignored pairs; returns the same shape with ignored entries removed; tuples whose globs list becomes empty are dropped. In orch/daemon/batch_manager.py after the find_blocking_items call (~line 462), query the BatchOverlapIgnore table for the current (project_id, batch_id, held_item_id), build the set of ignored pairs, and call the helper. If the filtered list is empty AND `blocked_by` was non-empty, emit a `batch_overlap_allowed_by_ignore` DaemonEvent with event_metadata={candidate_item_id, ignored_pairs:[{blocking_item_id, file_pattern}, ...]}; otherwise emit the existing item_held_for_scope events only for the surviving entries. Add `tdd_red_evidence` for the unit test in tests/unit/test_daemon_overlap_filter.py.

Per-agent review of S04: helper is pure (no DB, no logger side effects); batch_manager queries the ignore table with the project_id+batch_id+held_item_id triple (NOT held_item_id alone — verify with grep); the existing item_held_for_scope event emission path still works when no ignores exist (no behavioural regression); the new event type `batch_overlap_allowed_by_ignore` is registered in the canonical event-type list if there is one; the daemon's commit pattern matches the existing site (one commit per item, not per event); thread/lock safety vs the existing _emit_overlap_allowed_by_policy_if_needed branch.

Add two POST endpoints. (1) POST /project/{project_id}/batch/{batch_id}/overlap/{held_item_id}/ignore — body fields blocking_item_id, file_pattern, optional reason. Insert BatchOverlapIgnore via INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (use sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.insert with on_conflict_do_nothing). Emit `batch_overlap_ignored_by_operator` DaemonEvent (entity_type='work_item', entity_id=held_item_id). Return HTTP 200 with an empty fragment string `''`. (2) POST .../ignore-all — query the most recent 300s window of item_held_for_scope DaemonEvents for held_item_id, expand to (blocking_item_id, file_pattern) pairs, insert one ignore row per pair via the same on-conflict-do-nothing pattern, emit a single `batch_overlap_ignore_all_by_operator` event with event_metadata={count, pairs}. Return HTTP 200 with empty fragment (modal closes via outerHTML swap of #overlap-modal-root). Additionally: modify the existing GET /project/{p}/batch/{b}/overlap/{h} endpoint from CR-00077 to filter out file globs already present in batch_overlap_ignore — query the table early and pass a set into group_overlap_events (or filter inside the route). Update the Timeline rendering function in dashboard/routers/batches.py to recognise the 3 new event types and render the lines per CR-00078 §5. Use the placeholder string "operator" for ignored_by with a `# TODO(auth):` comment.

Per-agent review of S06: both POST endpoints idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING verified, not a try/except swallow); ignore-all reads the 300s window (consistent with GET endpoint and _get_scope_statuses); event payloads carry enough audit detail (the file_pattern, the blocking_item_id, the holding item, the count for ignore-all); the GET endpoint correctly filters ignored files; Timeline lines escape user-controlled text (item_ids are project-controlled so low risk, but file_pattern is) — Jinja autoescape suffices but verify in the templated text helper; placeholder `ignored_by="operator"` is a single literal with a clear TODO; no DB writes outside these endpoints.

Extend dashboard/templates/fragments/batch_overlap_modal.html (introduced by CR-00077) — for each `<li>` in the file list, add the Ignore button per design §2 with hx-post + hx-vals + hx-target='closest .iw-modal-file-row' hx-swap='outerHTML'. The `<li>` must gain class `iw-modal-file-row`. At the bottom of `.iw-modal-body`, add a `<footer class="iw-modal-footer">` containing the master button per §3 with hx-post .../ignore-all + hx-target='#overlap-modal-root' hx-swap='innerHTML' + hx-confirm. Append CSS to dashboard/static/styles.css for `.iw-modal-ignore-btn` (small, right-aligned in row), `.iw-modal-ignore-all-btn` (primary-styled, footer placement), `.iw-modal-footer` (border-top, padding, right-align), `.iw-modal-file-row` (flex layout for the glob + button). Do NOT modify the read-only 404 branch — when `empty=true` from CR-00077, there are no files and no master button is rendered.

Per-agent review of S08: Ignore buttons are inside the `<li>`s and use hx-target='closest .iw-modal-file-row' (NOT a global selector); master button is inside `.iw-modal-footer` and present only when sections list is non-empty (no master button on the 404/empty path); CSS classes use `iw-modal-*` prefix (no collisions); confirm dialog text is meaningful (`Ignore every remaining overlap for X in this batch and let it start?`); no Tailwind input edits; no `str.format`-style filter calls in the template.

Four new test files. (1) tests/unit/test_daemon_overlap_filter.py — filter_blocked_by_ignores: empty ignores returns input unchanged; full ignore returns empty; partial ignore drops only matching pairs; tuple with all globs ignored is dropped from result; exact string equality on glob (no fnmatch). (2) tests/unit/test_batch_overlap_ignore.py — model instantiation, repr, composite PK uniqueness (sqlite-compat or testcontainer). (3) tests/integration/test_batch_overlap_ignore_flow.py — seed Project+Batch+held WorkItem+blocking WorkItem+BatchItem+two item_held_for_scope events; pre-populate BatchOverlapIgnore with one pair; invoke the batch_manager's poll path (or the helper that exercises it) on a testcontainer; assert remaining blocking pair still holds (AC4); pre-populate ALL pairs as ignored; re-invoke; assert item is launched + batch_overlap_allowed_by_ignore event emitted (AC3). Also a per-batch isolation case (AC5): ignore in BATCH-A leaves BATCH-B held. (4) tests/dashboard/test_batch_overlap_ignore_endpoints.py — POST /ignore inserts row + emits event (assert exact event_type string + exact event_metadata payload); POST /ignore twice → no duplicate row but second event still emitted (AC2 idempotency); POST /ignore-all inserts N rows + emits ignore-all event with count=N; GET modal after partial ignore filters out ignored files (assert ignored glob NOT in response.text); a Timeline-rendering test (AC6) that seeds one DaemonEvent of each of the 3 new event types and asserts the exact §5 human-readable line for each. Plus: extend tests/dashboard/test_batch_overlap_modal.py if needed so the existing happy-path test still passes when an ignore row is present (it should — the filter is server-side). Capture tdd_red_evidence for each new module.

Per-agent review of S10: assertion strength (exact event_type strings, exact glob strings, NOT just response.ok); per-batch isolation case (AC5) actually creates two batches and asserts behavior on BATCH-B remains unchanged; testcontainer fixture used (NEVER live DB); idempotency case asserts a row count of 1 after two POSTs to the same endpoint (count, not boolean); tdd_red_evidence shows AssertionError or 404-vs-200 mismatch.

Global cross-agent review across S01..S11: every AC1-AC8 has at least one verifying artifact (test or browser_verification step); scope discipline — git diff origin/main -- executor/ MUST be empty; orch/ diff confined to models.py, db/migrations/, daemon/batch_manager.py, and the new daemon/overlap_ignore.py (if created); the new migration's downgrade() works (make migration-check at S03 passed); the modal partial extension does not break CR-00077's happy-path tests; the read-only contract from CR-00077 — opening the modal without any operator action — still does NOT write to the database (verify by inspecting the GET endpoint).
QV: Linting
QV: Formatting
QV: Type checking
QV: Unit tests
QV: Integration tests
QV: Assertion scanner

QV: Browser verification — open modal on a held item, click Ignore on one file (row disappears), reopen modal (ignored file is gone), click Ignore all & start (modal closes), confirm via the Items tab list that the held item transitions out of Held within one poll interval (~60s). No regression check on the GET modal endpoint for items that have no ignores.

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Focus: (1) whether the daemon-hook isolation held — the helper stayed pure and the batch_manager change was minimal; (2) whether the per-batch isolation case (AC5) was tested with two distinct batches; (3) whether the modal partial extension preserved CR-00077's happy-path tests; (4) cost analysis: did any fix cycle trace back to a misunderstanding of the ON CONFLICT semantics or the 300s window inheritance from CR-00077?
